author: deme1744
content:
Regarding this, I just checked go-ethereum and the contract deployment is also implemented there: https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/master/consensus/misc/eip7997.go#L40
It makes sense also as what else is the point of that EIP. 

I didn't check other clients.

author: jsign
content:
<@892053833121923094> do you have some opinion about this discussion?

TL;DR a bit to help reading all history necessarily:
- 7997 implemented in the eest specs does not deploy the contract since we already know it exists in mainnet (AFAIK, please correct me if wrong)
- Nimbus and apparently Geth do not do what the spec says reg deploying the contract if doesn't exist. They do a lookup and deploy it if necessary.

That last point is technically a state lookup access that gets visible in the executionWitness construction, and different from EEST executionWitness since that "deploy if necessary" apparently isn't there.

LMK your thoughts about this!
